## Summary

At the time of purchase (June 2018), Carlson was in the middle of a Class A Advanced season with Palm Beach, slashing .247/.345/.386 with nine home runs and 53 RBI in 99 games—respectable but not exceptional numbers that suggested he was still developing. However, he had been widely viewed as an average prospect with room to grow given his physical frame, and hadn't posted an OPS above .738 in his first three professional seasons, though his wRC+ indicated above-average hitting ability for his leagues. The investment succeeded because Carlson had a dramatic breakout in 2019 with the Springfield Cardinals, slashing .281/.364/.518 with 21 home runs, 59 RBI, and 18 stolen bases over 108 games before promotion to Memphis, which made him rise from outside the top-100 prospects to the No. 24 overall prospect and earned him the MiLB.com Breakout Prospect of the Year award. He subsequently made his MLB debut on August 15, 2020, and became the starting right fielder in 2021 and was a finalist for National League Rookie of the Year, validating
